title: Design a stateful streaming job for recovery
description: A path from defining keys and state to checkpointing and recovery testing before the job carries production load.
body: Use when building a job that keeps state across events. Do not wire operators and ship — without a recovery plan the first failure loses or duplicates data. Actions: define keys and state with TTL, enable checkpointing to durable storage, choose the restart and state-backend strategy, then chaos-test by killing a task and confirming clean recovery. Validation signals include a forced-restart test that resumes from a checkpoint with consistent output and a bounded state-size trend. Risk boundary: an untested recovery path fails when it matters most; if a kill test cannot recover cleanly, treat the job as not production-ready and escalate before deployment.
